threshold_event_that_counts: One additional local security row gets a canonical AVID report or vulnerability identifier that matches the same concrete incident rather than only same-platform or product-family analogy.

current_safe_publication_state_now: AVID is currently the only comparator family with two canonical direct local joins (`LIMEN-000002 -> AVID-2026-R1495`; `LIMEN-000016 -> AVID-2026-R1707`), but interoperability still remains replicated-yet-thin across 11 seeded security rows with 9 explicit non-direct rows.
publication_delta_if_threshold_lands: Highest-value shard-002 threshold remains one third exact AVID security join, because the package is already past singleton proof-of-concept and the next meaningful delta is stronger replication without widening beyond security rows.
core_denominator_or_sidecar_effect: No six-source denominator change, but the AVID sidecar now reads as 2-direct / 9-non-direct; another exact join would strengthen the replicated-exactness story rather than create it for the first time.

what_stays_bounded_even_if_resolved: Does not justify treating AVID as a general incident census, promoting analogue or placeholder rows, or inferring that non-security comparators have comparable machine-readiness.

publication_delta_if_threshold_lands: Second-highest shard-002 threshold: one exact case-study ID would change the publication story from pure graph-overlay support to mixed graph-plus-case identity, materially improving interoperability nuance without requiring broad corpus growth.

what_stays_bounded_even_if_resolved: Does not justify presenting ATLAS as a chronology source, universal incident-ID registry, or broad same-case bridge across the whole security package.
